ddls:
CREATE TABLE "system_calls" (
  "call_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system call */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the system call was executed */,
  "call_type" TEXT /* Type of system call (e.g., accept, close, ctl) */,
  "execution_time" REAL /* Execution time of the system call */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server where the call was executed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who initiated the system call */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the system call (e.g., successful, failed) */,
  "resource_usage" TEXT /* Resources used during the system call (e.g., CPU, memory) */,
  "call_parameters" TEXT /* Parameters passed to the system call */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("call_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_calls_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_calls_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_calls_call_type FOREIGN KEY ("call_type") REFERENCES call_types ("type_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name of the server */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the server */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the server */,
  "os_version" TEXT /* Operating system version of the server */,
  "hardware_config" TEXT /* Hardware configuration of the server */,
  "last_reboot" TEXT /* Last time the server was rebooted */,
  "uptime" INTEGER /* Uptime of the server in seconds */,
  "maintenance_window" TEXT /* Maintenance window for the server */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "call_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each call type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the call type (e.g., accept, close, ctl) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the call type */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the system call (e.g., I/O, network, process management) */,
  "criticality" INTEGER /* Criticality level of the system call (1 for high, 5 for low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "execution_times" (
  "time_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each execution time entry */,
  "call_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system call */,
  "execution_time" REAL /* Execution time of the system call */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the execution time was recorded */,
  "call_status" TEXT /* Status of the execution (e.g., successful, failed) */,
  "exception_details" TEXT /* Exception messages or errors that occurred during execution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("time_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_execution_times_call_id FOREIGN KEY ("call_id") REFERENCES system_calls ("call_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "server_calls" (
  "call_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system call */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server where the call was executed */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the call was executed */,
  "call_status" TEXT /* Status of the system call (e.g., successful, failed) */,
  "call_parameters" TEXT /* Parameters passed to the system call */,
  "resource_usage" TEXT /* Resources used during the system call (e.g., CPU, memory) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("call_id", "server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, engineer, analyst) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last time the user logged in */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "call_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system call being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string for the access request */,
  "access_duration" REAL /* Duration of the access event in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_call_id FOREIGN KEY ("call_id") REFERENCES system_calls ("call_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "call_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system call that triggered the alert */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server where the alert was triggered */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was triggered */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., high execution time, unusual pattern) */,
  "alert_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the alert */,
  "alert_severity" INTEGER /* Severity level of the alert (1 for high, 5 for low) */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Indicates if the alert has been resolved */,
  "resolution_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_call_id FOREIGN KEY ("call_id") REFERENCES system_calls ("call_id")
)
